title: update writes no in-block diagnostic — a blocked schema's failures appear only in a CLI report, not in the block file
description: On a blocked schema update writes nothing into the block: the failure is visible only in a CLI report, and the blocked contract leaves the block byte-unchanged (index.ts:491-508). The operator opens the block and sees no marking of the offending items or fields.

evidence.reference: the byte-unchanged blocked contract — the block is never written on the blocked path
writeBlockForDir is the whole-block writer; identity-stamping recomputes content_hash and pre-write AJV rejects non-schema marker fields, so in-file markers are not free
putObject/getObject — the as-installed block/schema body is recoverable, so 'no damage' can hold without 'never written'

impact: A blocked schema's failures are not visible at the point of fix — in the block file — only in a separate report the operator must cross-reference by hand.
proposed_resolution: git-merge-style failure markers written into the block at the offending items/fields, with the pre-marker block recoverable from the object store. This supersedes the byte-unchanged blocked contract with a marker-mode (the operator's git model: markers in-file, fix, resolve via the blocked resolution loop). Pairs with the blocked resolution loop and DEC-0018.
